name: [Meal Prep] Error Alert -> Telegram
_setup_notes: NOT triggered directly -- it is a target, not a flow you run yourself. After importing it:
1. Open EACH of the other three workflows (Inbox, Digest, Replies) -> the 3-dot menu -> Settings
-> 'Error Workflow' -> select '[Meal Prep] Error Alert -> Telegram'. Repeat for all three.
2. Fill REPLACE_WITH_YOUR_TELEGRAM_USER_ID with your numeric Telegram id (same value used in the
3. Confirm the Telegram node's credential is 'Telegram Bot - Meal Prep' (it should bind correctly
on import since only one credential of that name exists, but check anyway -- see OPERATOR.md's
'n8n binds credentials by ID, not by name' trap).
Deliberately plain text (no parse_mode) -- a raw error message or stack trace can contain
underscores/asterisks/brackets that break Telegram's Markdown parser, which would make the alert
itself silently fail to send. The whole point of this workflow is to never be the thing that goes
silent, so it trades formatting for reliability.
This workflow existing is what would have caught the 2026-07 GitHub PAT outage (D-049) on the
FIRST failed capture instead of three days of silence -- n8n already knew every failure via its
own execution log, nothing was surfacing it to Telegram where it would actually get seen.
One Error Workflow per app, not shared across apps -- it reuses that app's own Telegram credential
so the alert arrives on the bot you already associate with that app, and keeps this workflow from
becoming its own cross-app shared-credential single point of failure.
